name: abstain_low_confidence
description: Implicit-abstain gate at engine/assessor.py:1075-1094 — even a validator-approved Compliant verdict is demoted to needs_review when the LLM self-reports confidence below CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD (0.35 as of 2026-06-06, see engine/assessor.py:374). The case sets confidence=0.2, well under the floor. Narrative must pass the validator first (use 'examined' / 'confirmed via' / 'documented in' affirming phrases) so we exercise the post-validation abstain gate, not the pre-validation classification path. Expected per Fix 3 (KERNEL_VERSION 0.5.0): status is coerced to None on hard-abstain (so the reviewer cannot accidentally ship an unverified verdict), and the LLM's intended Compliant guess is preserved on proposed_status for the reviewer UI / calibration export. Pinning the threshold here means a future lowering of CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD past 0.2 would silently flip this case to a Compliant verdict — the failure is the desired signal.

ccis_row.guidance: The organization establishes conditions for group and role membership.
ccis_row.procedures: Examine documented conditions for group / role membership.

tagged_evidence:
## Tagged evidence
- USD00077777 Example System Access Control Plan Rev A — mentions group membership in passing; does not enumerate conditions or approvers.

llm_stub_proposals.narrative: Examined USD00077777 Example System Access Control Plan Rev A; confirmed via section 2 that conditions for group and role membership are documented in the plan.
